question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $55 by end of January?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of January 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

question: Will Elon Musk post 0-19 tweets from December 30 to January 6, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from December 30 12:00 PM ET to January 6, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

question: Big Bash League: Brisbane Heat vs Melbourne Stars (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Brisbane Heat and Melbourne Stars scheduled for January 2 2026 in Big Bash League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: Big Bash League: Brisbane Heat vs Melbourne Stars (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Brisbane Heat Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Brisbane Heat and Melbourne Stars scheduled for 2026-01-02 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Brisbane Heat will be considered correct if  Brisbane Heat is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Stars will be considered correct if Melbourne Stars is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Big Bash League: Brisbane Heat vs Melbourne Stars (Game 1) - Most Sixes Brisbane Heat Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Brisbane Heat and Melbourne Stars scheduled for 2026-01-02 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Brisbane Heat will be considered correct if Brisbane Heat is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Melbourne Stars.The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Stars will be considered correct if Melbourne Stars is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Brisbane Heat.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Brisbane Heat will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Brisbane Heat.
The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Stars will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Melbourne Stars.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: SA20: Paarl Royals vs MI Cape Town (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Paarl Royals and MI Cape Town scheduled for January 2 2026 in SA20.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: SA20: Paarl Royals vs MI Cape Town (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Paarl Royals Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Paarl Royals and MI Cape Town scheduled for 2026-01-02 in SA20.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Paarl Royals will be considered correct if  Paarl Royals is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to MI Cape Town will be considered correct if MI Cape Town is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Spread: BV Borussia 09 Dortmund (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for January 9 at 2:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "BV Borussia 09 Dortmund" if BV Borussia 09 Dortmund win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Eintracht Frankfurt".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

question: Will Trump admin release any more Epstein related files by January 9?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Trump Administration publicly releases any files pertaining to Jeffrey Epstein by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any release will qualify, including re-releases, partial releases, unsubstantive materials, or documents whose contents were already known from prior reporting.

Qualifying releases must be made by the Executive Branch itself, such as through an official government website, press release, or FOIA reading room. Transmissions solely to courts, Congress, or other non-Executive entities do not qualify unless the Executive Branch subsequently makes the files publicly accessible.

The following will not qualify:
-Announcements of releases that are not implemented within this market’s timeframe.
-Releases made exclusively by courts, Congress or its committees, state or local governments, private entities, or leaks not officially attributable to the Executive Branch.
-Leaked, unofficial, or anonymously sourced disclosures.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Big Bash League: Sydney Thunder vs Hobart Hurricanes (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Sydney Thunder and Hobart Hurricanes scheduled for January 3 2026 in Big Bash League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: Big Bash League: Sydney Thunder vs Hobart Hurricanes (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Sydney Thunder Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Sydney Thunder and Hobart Hurricanes scheduled for 2026-01-03 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Sydney Thunder will be considered correct if  Sydney Thunder is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Hobart Hurricanes will be considered correct if Hobart Hurricanes is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Big Bash League: Sydney Thunder vs Hobart Hurricanes (Game 1) - Most Sixes Sydney Thunder Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Sydney Thunder and Hobart Hurricanes scheduled for 2026-01-03 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Sydney Thunder will be considered correct if Sydney Thunder is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Hobart Hurricanes.The outcome corresponding to Hobart Hurricanes will be considered correct if Hobart Hurricanes is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Sydney Thunder.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: SA20: Pretoria Capitals vs Durban's Super Giants (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Pretoria Capitals and Durban's Super Giants scheduled for January 3 2026 in SA20.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: SA20: Pretoria Capitals vs Durban's Super Giants (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Pretoria Capitals Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Pretoria Capitals and Durban's Super Giants scheduled for 2026-01-03 in SA20.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Pretoria Capitals will be considered correct if  Pretoria Capitals is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Durban's Super Giants will be considered correct if Durban's Super Giants is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

description:

The outcome corresponding to Joburg Super Kings will be considered correct if Joburg Super Kings is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Sunrisers Eastern Cape.The outcome corresponding to Sunrisers Eastern Cape will be considered correct if Sunrisers Eastern Cape is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Joburg Super Kings.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".

description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Sylhet Strikers and Rangpur Riders scheduled for 2026-01-02 in BPL T20.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Sylhet Strikers will be considered correct if Sylhet Strikers is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Rangpur Riders.The outcome corresponding to Rangpur Riders will be considered correct if Rangpur Riders is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Sylhet Strikers.

question: BPL: Durbar Rajshahi vs Rangpur Riders (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Durbar Rajshahi and Rangpur Riders scheduled for January 1 2026 in BPL.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

question: BPL: Sylhet Strikers vs Durdanto Dhaka (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Sylhet Strikers and Durdanto Dhaka scheduled for January 1 2026 in BPL.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: BPL: Sylhet Strikers vs Durdanto Dhaka (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Sylhet Strikers Winner
description:

If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.

description:

This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Durdanto Dhaka and Rangpur Riders scheduled for 2025-12-30 in BPL T20.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Durdanto Dhaka will be considered correct if Durdanto Dhaka is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Rangpur Riders.The outcome corresponding to Rangpur Riders will be considered correct if Rangpur Riders is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Durdanto Dhaka.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: BPL: Durdanto Dhaka vs Rangpur Riders (Game 1) - Team Top Batter Durdanto Dhaka Winner
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Durdanto Dhaka and Rangpur Riders scheduled for 2025-12-30 in BPL T20.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Durdanto Dhaka will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Durdanto Dhaka.
The outcome corresponding to Rangpur Riders will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Rangpur Riders.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: BPL: Sylhet Strikers vs Chattogram Challengers (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Sylhet Strikers and Chattogram Challengers scheduled for December 30 2025 in BPL.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Sylhet Strikers and Chattogram Challengers scheduled for 2025-12-30 in BPL T20.

If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

question: BPL: Sylhet Strikers vs Chattogram Challengers (Game 1) - Completed match?
description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Sylhet Strikers and Chattogram Challengers scheduled for 2025-12-30 in BPL T20 is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.

question: BPL: Sylhet Strikers vs Chattogram Challengers (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Sylhet Strikers Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Sylhet Strikers and Chattogram Challengers scheduled for 2025-12-30 in BPL T20.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Sylhet Strikers will be considered correct if  Sylhet Strikers is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Chattogram Challengers will be considered correct if Chattogram Challengers is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: BPL: Noakhali vs Durbar Rajshahi (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Noakhali and Durbar Rajshahi scheduled for December 29 2025 in BPL.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: BPL: Noakhali vs Durbar Rajshahi (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Noakhali Winner
description:

This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Noakhali will be considered correct if  Noakhali is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Durbar Rajshahi will be considered correct if Durbar Rajshahi is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.

If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

description:

This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Chattogram Challengers and Rangpur Riders scheduled for 2025-12-29 in BPL T20.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Chattogram Challengers will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Chattogram Challengers.
The outcome corresponding to Rangpur Riders will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Rangpur Riders.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.
